author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
mṛta śiśu la’ye tabe bhakatavatsala
bhakata⋅saṅgete gāya śrī⋅nāma⋅maṅgala

gāite gāite gelā jāhnavīra tīre
bālake satkāra kaila jāhnavīra nīre

jāhnavī balena, mama saubhāgya apāra
saphala hoila vrata chila je āmāra

mṛta śiśu dena gorā jāhnavīra jale
uthali jāhnavī-devī śiśu laya kole

uthaliyā sparśe gorā⋅caraṇa⋅kamala
śiśu⋅kole preme devī haya ṭalamala

jāhnavīra bhāva dekhi’ jata bhaktagaṇa
śrī⋅nāma⋅maṅgaladhvani kare anukṣaṇa

svarga haite deve kare puṣpa⋅variṣaṇa
vimāna⋅saṅkula tabe chāila gagana

eirūpe nānā bhāve hoiyā magana
satkāra kariyā snāna kaila sarva-jana

parama⋅ānande sabe gela nija ghare
bhaktivinoda maje gorā⋅bhāvabhare
en_translation:
Then, taking the dead child, the Lord, who is affectionate to His devotees, sings the auspicious holy name with them.

While singing, he went to the bank of the Ganges and performed the funeral rites for the child in its waters.

The Jāhnavī says, "My good fortune is boundless; my vow has become successful."

Gorā gives the dead child into the waters of the Ganges. Swelling up, Jāhnavī-devī takes the child into her lap.

Swelling up, she touches Gorā's lotus feet. With the child in her lap, the goddess trembles with divine love.

Seeing the emotional state of the Jāhnavī, all the devotees continuously make the auspicious sound of the holy name.

From heaven, the demigods shower flowers. Then the sky becomes covered, filled with celestial airplanes.

In this way, becoming absorbed in various emotional states, all the people performed the funeral rites and then took a bath.

In supreme bliss, all went to their homes. Bhaktivinoda is immersed in the fullness of the mood of Gorā.
